text.title: Terraform Core Concepts
text.summary: HCL building blocks, workflow, meta-arguments, lifecycle rules, and state management fundamentals.

rule.statement: Use the standard 8-block structure: terraform, provider, resource, data, variable, output, locals, module — each with a distinct responsibility.
rule.rationale: Mixing responsibilities (e.g., embedding logic in resource blocks that belongs in locals) makes configurations hard to refactor and test.

rule.statement: Always use pessimistic version constraints (~> major.minor) for providers; pin Terraform itself to >= 1.5.0 minimum.
rule.rationale: Pessimistic constraints allow patch updates automatically while blocking breaking major changes. TF 1.5+ is required for import blocks.

section.title: Terraform Workflow

rule.statement: Follow the four-step workflow in order: init → plan → apply → destroy (never skip plan before apply in automation).
rule.rationale: Skipping plan in CI allows unexpected destroys or replacements to reach production without review.

section.note: Commands: terraform init (initialize + download providers), terraform plan (dry run), terraform apply (execute), terraform destroy (teardown).

rule.statement: Use for_each over count when resources need unique identifiers or will be addressed individually; use count only for identical, index-based resources.
rule.rationale: count creates resources addressed by index; removing an item from the middle shifts indexes and causes unexpected replacements. for_each uses stable keys.

rule.statement: Add explicit depends_on only when implicit dependency graphs are insufficient; avoid overuse as it disables parallel operations.
rule.rationale: Terraform builds dependency graphs from references automatically. Explicit depends_on is a last resort that serializes operations unnecessarily.

rule.statement: Set create_before_destroy on resources where downtime is unacceptable; set prevent_destroy on production databases and state backends.
rule.rationale: Default destroy-then-create causes downtime. prevent_destroy adds a guardrail against accidental terraform destroy on critical data stores.

rule.statement: Use precondition and postcondition blocks to validate assumptions before and after resource creation rather than relying on cloud API errors.
rule.rationale: Conditions surface misconfigurations at plan time with clear error messages, before any resources are created or modified.

rule.statement: Always configure a remote backend with locking; add *.tfstate* to .gitignore; commit .terraform.lock.hcl for provider version consistency.
rule.rationale: Local state blocks team collaboration and has no locking. State files contain sensitive data and must never be committed to git.
